mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output that improves short-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes falls below 0.02 during easing periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising unemployment confirms economic contraction that destroys equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial output and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that lifts equity prices, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60 percent of periods where both series register positive month-over-month changes.
